message:
refactor(mcp): with_mutation closures return McpResult; minor tools.rs fixes; refresh help snapshot

src/mcp/mod.rs::BeadsState::with_mutation
  Change the closure bound from
      F: FnMut(&mut SqliteStorage) -> crate::Result<R>
  to
      F: FnMut(&mut SqliteStorage) -> fastmcp_rust::McpResult<R>
  and update the return type to `fastmcp_rust::McpResult<R>` to
  match. Each tool callback already needs to construct
  protocol-shaped errors (id-not-found surfaces, validation
  details, etc.) so previously every callsite paid a small
  `to_mcp` wrapper at the boundary. Pushing `McpResult` all the
  way through means the closures can return their own
  protocol errors directly; the wrapper site collapses from
      let result = f(&mut storage).map_err(to_mcp)?;
  to
      let result = f(&mut storage)?;
  with `to_mcp` only retained for the `open_storage()` step that
  surfaces a generic crate error.

src/mcp/tools.rs (CreateIssueTool, UpdateIssueTool, CloseIssueTool)
  Adapt the closures to the new bound:
    - storage.{next_child_number, create_issue, update_issue,
      get_issue_details}? → `.map_err(beads_to_mcp)?` so a bare
      crate::Error becomes an McpError with the same surface as the
      rest of the tool's protocol errors.
    - Refactor the `if let Some(details) = ... && details.issue.status == Closed`
      chain in CloseIssueTool back into nested `if let` /
      `if status == Closed` so the early-return arm short-circuits
      cleanly under the new error type.
    - `priority.as_str()` → `priority.0` in the JSON result, matching
      the new Priority shape used elsewhere in the response.
    - Clone the `coercions` Vec before extending `warnings` (the
      borrow checker now sees the original move conflict because the
      closure result type changed).
    - Clone `issue_type` when used both in the `Issue` literal and
      later in the response JSON.

tests/snapshots/snapshots__snapshots__cli_output__help_output.snap
  Pick up the `serve  Start an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server
  on stdio` line that now appears between `update` and `upgrade` in
  the alphabetised command list (insta also rewrote the
  `assertion_line: 11` header). Snapshot-only — no behavior change.

files.patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 ---
 source: tests/snapshots/cli_output.rs
+assertion_line: 11
 expression: normalize_output(&output.stdout)
 ---
 ID-REDACTED issue tracker (SQLite + JSONL)
@@ -43,6 +44,7 @@ Commands:
   sync         Sync database with JSONL file (export or import)
   undefer      Undefer issues (make ready again)
   update       Update an issue
+  serve        Start an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server on stdio
   upgrade      Upgrade br to the latest version
   version      Show version information
   where        Show the active .beads directory
